functions.name: capAdd
functions.description: Specifies a list of Linux capabilities to add to the container.
functions.remarks:
Linux capabilities allow fine-grained control of the privileges assigned to a process.
This property provides the ability to add specific capabilities to the set of capabilities available to the container.
Each capability should be specified as a string in the list. Use this property to enhance the container's permissions
beyond the default set provided by the Docker runtime, if required by the application running inside the container.

functions.name: capDrop
functions.description: Represents a list of Linux capabilities to be dropped from the service's container. This property can be used to restrict specific capabilities that the container should not have access to, enhancing security by implementing the principle of least privilege.

functions.name: cgroupParent
functions.description: Gets or sets the parent Cgroup for the container. This property defines the name of the Cgroup under which the container's resource constraints are managed.

functions.name: command
functions.description: Represents the command to override the default command specified in the image's Dockerfile. This property allows specifying how the container should run by defining an executable and its arguments.

functions.name: configs
functions.description: Represents a collection of configuration references associated with the service. Each configuration is defined as a reference to an external configuration resource, which can be used to manage application configurations.

functions.name: containerName
functions.description: Specifies the name of the container to be used. This property maps to the "container_name" field in a Docker Compose file. If set, the container will have the specified name; otherwise, a name will be automatically generated.

functions.name: dependsOn
functions.description: Specifies a list of services that this service depends on. The dependencies are expressed as service names with optional conditions. Supported conditions are: "service_started", "service_healthy", "service_completed_successfully" This property defines the order in which services should be started, ensuring that the specified services are initialized before the current service.

functions.name: devices
functions.description: Represents a collection of device mappings for the service container. This property defines the host-to-container device paths in Docker.

functions.name: dns
functions.description: Gets or sets a list of custom DNS server IP addresses to be used by the service container.

functions.name: dnsSearch
functions.description: Specifies the domain search options for the service's container. This property allows you to define one or more domain search suffixes that will be appended to unqualified DNS queries performed by the container. Typically used to configure how DNS resolution should behave in specific network setups.

functions.name: domainName
functions.description: Represents the domain name of a service container.

functions.name: entrypoint
functions.description: Specifies the entrypoint to be used for the container. This property allows overriding the default entrypoint of the image and defines the executable or command that is run when the container starts.

functions.name: envFile
functions.description: Represents a collection of paths to environment variable files used by the service. These files contain key-value pairs of environment variables that will be loaded and applied to the service configuration at runtime.

functions.name: environment
functions.description: Represents a collection of environment variables for the service container.
functions.remarks:
The property allows for specifying environment variables as key-value pairs.
These variables can be used to configure the behavior of the container
or pass information to the application running inside the container.

functions.name: expose
functions.description: Gets or sets a list of ports to expose from the container without publishing them to the host machine. This property defines internal ports that the container makes available to linked services or other containers within the same network, but these ports are not accessible from outside the container’s network.

functions.name: externalLinks
functions.description: Gets or sets the external links for the service. External links are references to services defined outside the current Docker Compose file, enabling communication with containers in other projects or environments.

functions.name: extraHosts
functions.description: Represents additional hostname-to-IP mappings for the service. These mappings allow you to manually define hostnames and corresponding IP addresses, effectively augmenting the DNS resolution for the service's containers.

functions.name: groupAdd
functions.description: Gets or sets a list of additional group IDs to add to the container's process. This allows the container to have access to resources or permissions associated with the specified groups.

functions.name: hostname
functions.description: Gets or sets the hostname for the service container. This defines the hostname that will be assigned to the container and can be used for network identification within the container's network.

functions.name: image
functions.description: Specifies the Docker image to be used for the service.
functions.remarks:
The image refers to the identifier of a container image hosted in a registry.
This property is required if no build instructions are provided for the service.
It may include an optional tag or digest to specify a particular version of the image.
If omitted, Docker will default to the `latest` tag.

functions.name: init
functions.description: Indicates whether the init binary should be used as the container's init process. When set to true, the init process is used to ensure proper reaping of zombie processes and signal forwarding inside the container.

functions.name: ipc
functions.description: Gets or sets the IPC (Inter-Process Communication) mode for the service. This property determines how IPC namespaces are shared between containers and the host. It can be set to values such as "none", "host", or a specific container ID to share IPC resources with.

functions.name: isolation
functions.description: Specifies the isolation mode for the container. This property determines the level of isolation between the container and the host system. Common values include "default", "process", or "hyperv", and the supported options may depend on the container runtime or the platform being used.

functions.name: labels
functions.description: Represents a set of metadata labels for the service. These key-value pairs can be used to organize and identify objects within the service configuration.

functions.name: links
functions.description: Represents a service definition in a Docker Compose configuration file.

functions.name: macAddress
functions.description: Specifies a custom MAC (Media Access Control) address for the container's network interface.
functions.remarks:
A MAC address is a unique identifier assigned to a network interface controller (NIC).
Setting this property allows for specifying a predefined MAC address instead of letting the system assign one dynamically.
This can be useful for use cases where a consistent MAC address is required, such as DHCP reservations or specific network configurations.

functions.name: name
functions.description: Gets or sets the name of the Docker Compose member.
functions.remarks:
This property is used to uniquely identify the member (e.g., network, service) within
the Docker Compose configuration. It must be explicitly defined for all derived types.

functions.name: networkMode
functions.description: Specifies the network mode to be used for the container. This property determines the networking configuration, such as whether the container shares the network stack with the host, uses a predefined network, or operates in isolation. The value is typically a string that matches a network mode supported by the environment, such as 'bridge', 'host', 'none', or a custom network name.

functions.name: networks
functions.description: Defines the collection of networks that the service is connected to. This property specifies the names of the networks the service should be attached to. Each entry in this list represents a network defined in the Docker Compose file or an externally defined network. Connecting a service to one or more networks allows inter-service communication across those networks, as well as communication with external systems configured on those same networks. If no network is specified, the service is connected to the default network that is automatically created by Docker Compose for the project unless `network_mode` is set to another value.

functions.name: pid
functions.description: Gets or sets the PID (Process Identifier) namespace configuration for the container. This property determines whether the container shares the PID namespace with the host or other containers, allowing process visibility and signal sending between them.

functions.name: ports
functions.description: Represents a collection of port mappings for the service. Each mapping specifies how a container port is bound to a host port.

functions.name: privileged
functions.description: Indicates whether the container should run in privileged mode. When set to true, the container is granted extended Linux capabilities and device access.

functions.name: profiles
functions.description: Defines a list of profiles associated with the service. Profiles allow grouping of services and provide the ability to selectively enable services based on specified runtime profiles. If no profiles are specified, the service will be active in all configurations.

functions.name: pullPolicy
functions.description: Specifies the policy for pulling the Docker image.
functions.remarks:
This property determines when the container runtime should pull the image.
Common values include "always", "never", "missing", "if_not_present", or "build".
If not specified, the default behavior depends on the container runtime configuration.

functions.name: readOnly
functions.description: Defines whether the service containers should be run in read-only mode. If set to true, the containers will have a read-only file system, limiting write operations to specific directories defined by writable mounts or tempfs.

functions.name: restart
functions.description: Specifies the restart policy for the container. This property determines how the container should behave in case of a crash or termination. Common values include "no", "always", "on-failure", and "unless-stopped".

functions.name: secrets
functions.description: Represents a collection of secret references used by the service.
functions.remarks:
Each entry in the collection refers to a specific secret that is utilized by the service,
typically for managing sensitive information in a secure manner (e.g., credentials, tokens).

functions.name: securityOpt
functions.description: Represents a list of security options that can be applied to the container. This is used to configure security-related settings specific to the container such as SELinux labels or AppArmor profiles, providing fine-grained control over the container's security behavior.

functions.name: setCapAdd
functions.description: Specifies a list of Linux capabilities to add to the container.
functions.remarks:
Linux capabilities allow fine-grained control of the privileges assigned to a process.
This property provides the ability to add specific capabilities to the set of capabilities available to the container.
Each capability should be specified as a string in the list. Use this property to enhance the container's permissions
beyond the default set provided by the Docker runtime, if required by the application running inside the container.

functions.name: setCapDrop
functions.description: Represents a list of Linux capabilities to be dropped from the service's container. This property can be used to restrict specific capabilities that the container should not have access to, enhancing security by implementing the principle of least privilege.

functions.name: setCgroupParent
functions.description: Gets or sets the parent Cgroup for the container. This property defines the name of the Cgroup under which the container's resource constraints are managed.

functions.name: setCommand
functions.description: Represents the command to override the default command specified in the image's Dockerfile. This property allows specifying how the container should run by defining an executable and its arguments.

functions.name: setConfigs
functions.description: Represents a collection of configuration references associated with the service. Each configuration is defined as a reference to an external configuration resource, which can be used to manage application configurations.

functions.name: setContainerName
functions.description: Specifies the name of the container to be used. This property maps to the "container_name" field in a Docker Compose file. If set, the container will have the specified name; otherwise, a name will be automatically generated.

functions.name: setDependsOn
functions.description: Specifies a list of services that this service depends on. The dependencies are expressed as service names with optional conditions. Supported conditions are: "service_started", "service_healthy", "service_completed_successfully" This property defines the order in which services should be started, ensuring that the specified services are initialized before the current service.

functions.name: setDevices
functions.description: Represents a collection of device mappings for the service container. This property defines the host-to-container device paths in Docker.

functions.name: setDns
functions.description: Gets or sets a list of custom DNS server IP addresses to be used by the service container.

functions.name: setDnsSearch
functions.description: Specifies the domain search options for the service's container. This property allows you to define one or more domain search suffixes that will be appended to unqualified DNS queries performed by the container. Typically used to configure how DNS resolution should behave in specific network setups.

functions.name: setDomainName
functions.description: Represents the domain name of a service container.

functions.name: setEntrypoint
functions.description: Specifies the entrypoint to be used for the container. This property allows overriding the default entrypoint of the image and defines the executable or command that is run when the container starts.

functions.name: setEnvFile
functions.description: Represents a collection of paths to environment variable files used by the service. These files contain key-value pairs of environment variables that will be loaded and applied to the service configuration at runtime.

functions.name: setEnvironment
functions.description: Represents a collection of environment variables for the service container.
functions.remarks:
The property allows for specifying environment variables as key-value pairs.
These variables can be used to configure the behavior of the container
or pass information to the application running inside the container.

functions.name: setExpose
functions.description: Gets or sets a list of ports to expose from the container without publishing them to the host machine. This property defines internal ports that the container makes available to linked services or other containers within the same network, but these ports are not accessible from outside the container’s network.

functions.name: setExternalLinks
functions.description: Gets or sets the external links for the service. External links are references to services defined outside the current Docker Compose file, enabling communication with containers in other projects or environments.

functions.name: setExtraHosts
functions.description: Represents additional hostname-to-IP mappings for the service. These mappings allow you to manually define hostnames and corresponding IP addresses, effectively augmenting the DNS resolution for the service's containers.

functions.name: setGroupAdd
functions.description: Gets or sets a list of additional group IDs to add to the container's process. This allows the container to have access to resources or permissions associated with the specified groups.

functions.name: setHostname
functions.description: Gets or sets the hostname for the service container. This defines the hostname that will be assigned to the container and can be used for network identification within the container's network.

functions.name: setImage
functions.description: Specifies the Docker image to be used for the service.
functions.remarks:
The image refers to the identifier of a container image hosted in a registry.
This property is required if no build instructions are provided for the service.
It may include an optional tag or digest to specify a particular version of the image.
If omitted, Docker will default to the `latest` tag.

functions.name: setInit
functions.description: Indicates whether the init binary should be used as the container's init process. When set to true, the init process is used to ensure proper reaping of zombie processes and signal forwarding inside the container.

functions.name: setIpc
functions.description: Gets or sets the IPC (Inter-Process Communication) mode for the service. This property determines how IPC namespaces are shared between containers and the host. It can be set to values such as "none", "host", or a specific container ID to share IPC resources with.

functions.name: setIsolation
functions.description: Specifies the isolation mode for the container. This property determines the level of isolation between the container and the host system. Common values include "default", "process", or "hyperv", and the supported options may depend on the container runtime or the platform being used.

functions.name: setLabels
functions.description: Represents a set of metadata labels for the service. These key-value pairs can be used to organize and identify objects within the service configuration.

functions.name: setLinks
functions.description: Represents a service definition in a Docker Compose configuration file.

functions.name: setMacAddress
functions.description: Specifies a custom MAC (Media Access Control) address for the container's network interface.
functions.remarks:
A MAC address is a unique identifier assigned to a network interface controller (NIC).
Setting this property allows for specifying a predefined MAC address instead of letting the system assign one dynamically.
This can be useful for use cases where a consistent MAC address is required, such as DHCP reservations or specific network configurations.

functions.name: setName
functions.description: Gets or sets the name of the Docker Compose member.
functions.remarks:
This property is used to uniquely identify the member (e.g., network, service) within
the Docker Compose configuration. It must be explicitly defined for all derived types.

functions.name: setNetworkMode
functions.description: Specifies the network mode to be used for the container. This property determines the networking configuration, such as whether the container shares the network stack with the host, uses a predefined network, or operates in isolation. The value is typically a string that matches a network mode supported by the environment, such as 'bridge', 'host', 'none', or a custom network name.

functions.name: setNetworks
functions.description: Defines the collection of networks that the service is connected to. This property specifies the names of the networks the service should be attached to. Each entry in this list represents a network defined in the Docker Compose file or an externally defined network. Connecting a service to one or more networks allows inter-service communication across those networks, as well as communication with external systems configured on those same networks. If no network is specified, the service is connected to the default network that is automatically created by Docker Compose for the project unless `network_mode` is set to another value.

functions.name: setPid
functions.description: Gets or sets the PID (Process Identifier) namespace configuration for the container. This property determines whether the container shares the PID namespace with the host or other containers, allowing process visibility and signal sending between them.

functions.name: setPorts
functions.description: Represents a collection of port mappings for the service. Each mapping specifies how a container port is bound to a host port.

functions.name: setPrivileged
functions.description: Indicates whether the container should run in privileged mode. When set to true, the container is granted extended Linux capabilities and device access.

functions.name: setProfiles
functions.description: Defines a list of profiles associated with the service. Profiles allow grouping of services and provide the ability to selectively enable services based on specified runtime profiles. If no profiles are specified, the service will be active in all configurations.

functions.name: setPullPolicy
functions.description: Specifies the policy for pulling the Docker image.
functions.remarks:
This property determines when the container runtime should pull the image.
Common values include "always", "never", "missing", "if_not_present", or "build".
If not specified, the default behavior depends on the container runtime configuration.

functions.name: setReadOnly
functions.description: Defines whether the service containers should be run in read-only mode. If set to true, the containers will have a read-only file system, limiting write operations to specific directories defined by writable mounts or tempfs.

functions.name: setRestart
functions.description: Specifies the restart policy for the container. This property determines how the container should behave in case of a crash or termination. Common values include "no", "always", "on-failure", and "unless-stopped".

functions.name: setSecrets
functions.description: Represents a collection of secret references used by the service.
functions.remarks:
Each entry in the collection refers to a specific secret that is utilized by the service,
typically for managing sensitive information in a secure manner (e.g., credentials, tokens).

functions.name: setSecurityOpt
functions.description: Represents a list of security options that can be applied to the container. This is used to configure security-related settings specific to the container such as SELinux labels or AppArmor profiles, providing fine-grained control over the container's security behavior.

functions.name: setStdinOpen
functions.description: Indicates whether standard input (stdin) should remain open and be attached to the service container, even if no terminal is connected.

functions.name: setStopGracePeriod
functions.description: Gets or sets the stop grace period for the container. This specifies the amount of time to wait before forcing a container to stop after the stop or shutdown signal is sent. The value can be defined in a time duration format, such as "10s" for 10 seconds or "1m" for 1 minute.

functions.name: setStopSignal
functions.description: Specifies the signal that will be used to stop the container. This property allows you to define a custom stop signal other than the default SIGTERM.

functions.name: setSysctls
functions.description: Represents a set of kernel parameters, specified as key-value pairs, that can be applied to the container at runtime. This property allows customization of specific Linux kernel settings (sysctl parameters) for the container, enabling fine-tuned control over its behavior. Common use cases include tuning network parameters or configuring shared memory limits. Note: Supported kernel parameters will vary based on the Docker daemon and the host system. Unsupported parameters will result in an error. Example: Use this property to set parameters like `net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies` or `net.core.somaxconn`.

functions.name: setTmpfs
functions.description: Specifies a list of temporary file systems (tmpfs) to be mounted inside the container. Each entry represents a directory on the container's filesystem, mounted as a tmpfs, which resides in-memory and is typically used for ephemeral storage or caching purposes.

functions.name: setTty
functions.description: Specifies whether a pseudo-TTY (teletypewriter) should be allocated for the container. When set to true, it enables the container to run with an interactive terminal session.

functions.name: setUlimits
functions.description: Represents a collection of ulimit constraints for the service. Ulimits specify system resource limitations to be applied to the container, such as maximum number of open files or maximum stack size.

functions.name: setUser
functions.description: Specifies the user that the container will run as. The value can be set to a numeric UID, a string for the username, or a combination of both (e.g., "UID:GID").

functions.name: setVolumes
functions.description: Defines the list of volumes to be mounted into the service's container.
functions.remarks:
Volumes provide a mechanism for persisting data used by the service or for sharing data between the host and the container.
Each volume entry maps a source path on the host or an anonymous volume to a target path within the container.
This property can also include named volumes as defined in the Compose file's top-level `volumes` section.
Volumes can specify additional options such as read-only access or volume drivers.

functions.name: setWorkingDir
functions.description: Gets or sets the working directory of the container. Specifies the directory in which commands are run inside the container. Corresponds to the "working_dir" property in a Docker Compose file.

functions.name: stdinOpen
functions.description: Indicates whether standard input (stdin) should remain open and be attached to the service container, even if no terminal is connected.

functions.name: stopGracePeriod
functions.description: Gets or sets the stop grace period for the container. This specifies the amount of time to wait before forcing a container to stop after the stop or shutdown signal is sent. The value can be defined in a time duration format, such as "10s" for 10 seconds or "1m" for 1 minute.

functions.name: stopSignal
functions.description: Specifies the signal that will be used to stop the container. This property allows you to define a custom stop signal other than the default SIGTERM.

functions.name: sysctls
functions.description: Represents a set of kernel parameters, specified as key-value pairs, that can be applied to the container at runtime. This property allows customization of specific Linux kernel settings (sysctl parameters) for the container, enabling fine-tuned control over its behavior. Common use cases include tuning network parameters or configuring shared memory limits. Note: Supported kernel parameters will vary based on the Docker daemon and the host system. Unsupported parameters will result in an error. Example: Use this property to set parameters like `net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies` or `net.core.somaxconn`.

functions.name: tmpfs

functions.name: tty
functions.description: Specifies whether a pseudo-TTY (teletypewriter) should be allocated for the container. When set to true, it enables the container to run with an interactive terminal session.

functions.name: ulimits
functions.description: Represents a collection of ulimit constraints for the service. Ulimits specify system resource limitations to be applied to the container, such as maximum number of open files or maximum stack size.

functions.name: user
functions.description: Specifies the user that the container will run as. The value can be set to a numeric UID, a string for the username, or a combination of both (e.g., "UID:GID").

functions.name: volumes

Volumes can specify additional options such as read-only access or volume drivers.

functions.name: workingDir

functions.name: condition
functions.description: Gets or sets the condition under which the service should be started.

functions.description: Gets or sets the condition under which the service should be started.

functions.name: hard
functions.description: Gets or sets the hard limit for the resource control.
functions.remarks:
The hard limit defines the maximum value that cannot be exceeded.
It is usually set by an administrator and imposes a strict upper boundary.
This property can be null, indicating no hard limit is set.

functions.name: setHard
functions.description: Gets or sets the hard limit for the resource control.
functions.remarks:
The hard limit defines the maximum value that cannot be exceeded.
It is usually set by an administrator and imposes a strict upper boundary.
This property can be null, indicating no hard limit is set.

functions.name: setSoft
functions.description: Defines the soft limit for the Ulimit configuration. The soft limit is the value for resource restrictions that a process is allowed to increase up to the hard limit. This property is nullable, which indicates that this configuration might not be set.

functions.name: soft
functions.description: Defines the soft limit for the Ulimit configuration. The soft limit is the value for resource restrictions that a process is allowed to increase up to the hard limit. This property is nullable, which indicates that this configuration might not be set.

functions.name: driver
functions.description: Gets or sets the name of the driver used for the volume. The driver is responsible for managing the volume and its storage backend.

functions.name: driverOpts

functions.name: external

A value of `true` specifies that the volume is managed outside the scope of the current application or configuration. A value of `false`, or a null value, indicates that the volume is managed internally or by default behavior.

functions.name: labels

functions.name: readOnly
functions.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the volume is mounted as read-only.
functions.remarks:
When set to true, the volume will be mounted with read-only permissions, preventing modification
of the data within the container. This is commonly used to enforce data immutability for certain use cases.

capabilities.name: attachable
capabilities.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the network is attachable. When this property is set to true, containers can dynamically attach to the network at runtime. capabilities.name: driver
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the driver used for the network. The driver determines the networking implementation that the container network is based on. Examples include bridge, overlay, host, etc.

capabilities.name: driverOpts

capabilities.remarks:

capabilities.name: external

When set to true, the network is assumed to be pre-existing and not defined by the application's configuration. capabilities.name: ingress

capabilities.name: internal

capabilities.name: labels

handleTypes.description: Represents a Docker Compose service definition.
handleTypes.remarks:

such as image, container configuration, environment variables, networks, and more.
It is designed to map directly to the service properties defined in a Docker Compose YAML file.

capabilities.name: addVolume
capabilities.description: Adds a volume mount to a generated Docker Compose service.
capabilities.signature: addVolume(source: string, target: string, type?: string, isReadOnly?: boolean, configure?: (obj: Volume) => Promise<void>): Service

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: The source volume name or host path.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target path inside the container.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The Docker Compose volume type, such as `volume` or `bind`.

parameters.name: isReadOnly
parameters.description: A value indicating whether the volume is mounted as read-only.

parameters.name: configure
parameters.description: An optional callback for further volume customization.

capabilities.name: capAdd
capabilities.description: Specifies a list of Linux capabilities to add to the container.
capabilities.remarks:
Linux capabilities allow fine-grained control of the privileges assigned to a process.
This property provides the ability to add specific capabilities to the set of capabilities available to the container.
Each capability should be specified as a string in the list. Use this property to enhance the container's permissions
beyond the default set provided by the Docker runtime, if required by the application running inside the container.

capabilities.name: capDrop
capabilities.description: Represents a list of Linux capabilities to be dropped from the service's container. This property can be used to restrict specific capabilities that the container should not have access to, enhancing security by implementing the principle of least privilege.

capabilities.name: cgroupParent
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the parent Cgroup for the container. This property defines the name of the Cgroup under which the container's resource constraints are managed.

capabilities.name: command
capabilities.description: Represents the command to override the default command specified in the image's Dockerfile. This property allows specifying how the container should run by defining an executable and its arguments.

capabilities.name: configs
capabilities.description: Represents a collection of configuration references associated with the service. Each configuration is defined as a reference to an external configuration resource, which can be used to manage application configurations.

capabilities.name: containerName
capabilities.description: Specifies the name of the container to be used. This property maps to the "container_name" field in a Docker Compose file. If set, the container will have the specified name; otherwise, a name will be automatically generated.

capabilities.name: dependsOn
capabilities.description: Specifies a list of services that this service depends on. The dependencies are expressed as service names with optional conditions. Supported conditions are: "service_started", "service_healthy", "service_completed_successfully" This property defines the order in which services should be started, ensuring that the specified services are initialized before the current service.

capabilities.name: devices
capabilities.description: Represents a collection of device mappings for the service container. This property defines the host-to-container device paths in Docker.

capabilities.name: dns
capabilities.description: Gets or sets a list of custom DNS server IP addresses to be used by the service container.

capabilities.name: dnsSearch
capabilities.description: Specifies the domain search options for the service's container. This property allows you to define one or more domain search suffixes that will be appended to unqualified DNS queries performed by the container. Typically used to configure how DNS resolution should behave in specific network setups.

capabilities.name: domainName
capabilities.description: Represents the domain name of a service container.

capabilities.name: entrypoint
capabilities.description: Specifies the entrypoint to be used for the container. This property allows overriding the default entrypoint of the image and defines the executable or command that is run when the container starts.

capabilities.name: envFile
capabilities.description: Represents a collection of paths to environment variable files used by the service. These files contain key-value pairs of environment variables that will be loaded and applied to the service configuration at runtime.

capabilities.name: environment
capabilities.description: Represents a collection of environment variables for the service container.
capabilities.remarks:
The property allows for specifying environment variables as key-value pairs.
These variables can be used to configure the behavior of the container
or pass information to the application running inside the container.

capabilities.name: expose
capabilities.description: Gets or sets a list of ports to expose from the container without publishing them to the host machine. This property defines internal ports that the container makes available to linked services or other containers within the same network, but these ports are not accessible from outside the container’s network.

capabilities.name: externalLinks
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the external links for the service. External links are references to services defined outside the current Docker Compose file, enabling communication with containers in other projects or environments.

capabilities.name: extraHosts
capabilities.description: Represents additional hostname-to-IP mappings for the service. These mappings allow you to manually define hostnames and corresponding IP addresses, effectively augmenting the DNS resolution for the service's containers.

capabilities.name: groupAdd
capabilities.description: Gets or sets a list of additional group IDs to add to the container's process. This allows the container to have access to resources or permissions associated with the specified groups.

capabilities.name: hostname
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the hostname for the service container. This defines the hostname that will be assigned to the container and can be used for network identification within the container's network.

capabilities.name: image
capabilities.description: Specifies the Docker image to be used for the service.
capabilities.remarks:
The image refers to the identifier of a container image hosted in a registry.
This property is required if no build instructions are provided for the service.
It may include an optional tag or digest to specify a particular version of the image.
If omitted, Docker will default to the `latest` tag.

capabilities.name: init
capabilities.description: Indicates whether the init binary should be used as the container's init process. When set to true, the init process is used to ensure proper reaping of zombie processes and signal forwarding inside the container.

capabilities.name: ipc
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the IPC (Inter-Process Communication) mode for the service. This property determines how IPC namespaces are shared between containers and the host. It can be set to values such as "none", "host", or a specific container ID to share IPC resources with.

capabilities.name: isolation
capabilities.description: Specifies the isolation mode for the container. This property determines the level of isolation between the container and the host system. Common values include "default", "process", or "hyperv", and the supported options may depend on the container runtime or the platform being used.

capabilities.name: labels
capabilities.description: Represents a set of metadata labels for the service. These key-value pairs can be used to organize and identify objects within the service configuration.

capabilities.name: links
capabilities.description: Represents a service definition in a Docker Compose configuration file.

capabilities.name: macAddress
capabilities.description: Specifies a custom MAC (Media Access Control) address for the container's network interface.
capabilities.remarks:
A MAC address is a unique identifier assigned to a network interface controller (NIC).
Setting this property allows for specifying a predefined MAC address instead of letting the system assign one dynamically.
This can be useful for use cases where a consistent MAC address is required, such as DHCP reservations or specific network configurations.

capabilities.name: name
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the name of the Docker Compose member.
capabilities.remarks:
This property is used to uniquely identify the member (e.g., network, service) within
the Docker Compose configuration. It must be explicitly defined for all derived types.

capabilities.name: networkMode
capabilities.description: Specifies the network mode to be used for the container. This property determines the networking configuration, such as whether the container shares the network stack with the host, uses a predefined network, or operates in isolation. The value is typically a string that matches a network mode supported by the environment, such as 'bridge', 'host', 'none', or a custom network name.

capabilities.name: networks
capabilities.description: Defines the collection of networks that the service is connected to. This property specifies the names of the networks the service should be attached to. Each entry in this list represents a network defined in the Docker Compose file or an externally defined network. Connecting a service to one or more networks allows inter-service communication across those networks, as well as communication with external systems configured on those same networks. If no network is specified, the service is connected to the default network that is automatically created by Docker Compose for the project unless `network_mode` is set to another value.

capabilities.name: pid
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the PID (Process Identifier) namespace configuration for the container. This property determines whether the container shares the PID namespace with the host or other containers, allowing process visibility and signal sending between them.

capabilities.name: ports
capabilities.description: Represents a collection of port mappings for the service. Each mapping specifies how a container port is bound to a host port.

capabilities.name: privileged
capabilities.description: Indicates whether the container should run in privileged mode. When set to true, the container is granted extended Linux capabilities and device access.

capabilities.name: profiles
capabilities.description: Defines a list of profiles associated with the service. Profiles allow grouping of services and provide the ability to selectively enable services based on specified runtime profiles. If no profiles are specified, the service will be active in all configurations.

capabilities.name: pullPolicy
capabilities.description: Specifies the policy for pulling the Docker image.

Common values include "always", "never", "missing", "if_not_present", or "build".
If not specified, the default behavior depends on the container runtime configuration.

capabilities.name: readOnly
capabilities.description: Defines whether the service containers should be run in read-only mode. If set to true, the containers will have a read-only file system, limiting write operations to specific directories defined by writable mounts or tempfs.

capabilities.name: restart
capabilities.description: Specifies the restart policy for the container. This property determines how the container should behave in case of a crash or termination. Common values include "no", "always", "on-failure", and "unless-stopped".

capabilities.name: setProfiles
capabilities.description: Defines a list of profiles associated with the service. Profiles allow grouping of services and provide the ability to selectively enable services based on specified runtime profiles. If no profiles are specified, the service will be active in all configurations.

capabilities.name: setPullPolicy
capabilities.description: Specifies the policy for pulling the Docker image.
capabilities.remarks:
This property determines when the container runtime should pull the image.
Common values include "always", "never", "missing", "if_not_present", or "build".
If not specified, the default behavior depends on the container runtime configuration.

capabilities.name: setReadOnly
capabilities.description: Defines whether the service containers should be run in read-only mode. If set to true, the containers will have a read-only file system, limiting write operations to specific directories defined by writable mounts or tempfs.

capabilities.name: setRestart
capabilities.description: Specifies the restart policy for the container. This property determines how the container should behave in case of a crash or termination. Common values include "no", "always", "on-failure", and "unless-stopped".

capabilities.name: setSecrets
capabilities.description: Represents a collection of secret references used by the service.
capabilities.remarks:
Each entry in the collection refers to a specific secret that is utilized by the service,
typically for managing sensitive information in a secure manner (e.g., credentials, tokens).

capabilities.name: setSecurityOpt
capabilities.description: Represents a list of security options that can be applied to the container. This is used to configure security-related settings specific to the container such as SELinux labels or AppArmor profiles, providing fine-grained control over the container's security behavior.

capabilities.name: setStdinOpen
capabilities.description: Indicates whether standard input (stdin) should remain open and be attached to the service container, even if no terminal is connected.

capabilities.name: setStopGracePeriod
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the stop grace period for the container. This specifies the amount of time to wait before forcing a container to stop after the stop or shutdown signal is sent. The value can be defined in a time duration format, such as "10s" for 10 seconds or "1m" for 1 minute.

capabilities.name: setStopSignal
capabilities.description: Specifies the signal that will be used to stop the container. This property allows you to define a custom stop signal other than the default SIGTERM.

capabilities.name: setSysctls
capabilities.description: Represents a set of kernel parameters, specified as key-value pairs, that can be applied to the container at runtime. This property allows customization of specific Linux kernel settings (sysctl parameters) for the container, enabling fine-tuned control over its behavior. Common use cases include tuning network parameters or configuring shared memory limits. Note: Supported kernel parameters will vary based on the Docker daemon and the host system. Unsupported parameters will result in an error. Example: Use this property to set parameters like `net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies` or `net.core.somaxconn`.

capabilities.name: setTmpfs
capabilities.description: Specifies a list of temporary file systems (tmpfs) to be mounted inside the container. Each entry represents a directory on the container's filesystem, mounted as a tmpfs, which resides in-memory and is typically used for ephemeral storage or caching purposes.

capabilities.name: setTty
capabilities.description: Specifies whether a pseudo-TTY (teletypewriter) should be allocated for the container. When set to true, it enables the container to run with an interactive terminal session.

capabilities.name: setUlimits
capabilities.description: Represents a collection of ulimit constraints for the service. Ulimits specify system resource limitations to be applied to the container, such as maximum number of open files or maximum stack size.

capabilities.name: setUser
capabilities.description: Specifies the user that the container will run as. The value can be set to a numeric UID, a string for the username, or a combination of both (e.g., "UID:GID").

capabilities.name: setVolumes
capabilities.description: Defines the list of volumes to be mounted into the service's container.
capabilities.remarks:
Volumes provide a mechanism for persisting data used by the service or for sharing data between the host and the container.
Each volume entry maps a source path on the host or an anonymous volume to a target path within the container.
This property can also include named volumes as defined in the Compose file's top-level `volumes` section.
Volumes can specify additional options such as read-only access or volume drivers.

capabilities.name: setWorkingDir
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the working directory of the container. Specifies the directory in which commands are run inside the container. Corresponds to the "working_dir" property in a Docker Compose file.

capabilities.name: stdinOpen
capabilities.description: Indicates whether standard input (stdin) should remain open and be attached to the service container, even if no terminal is connected.

capabilities.name: stopGracePeriod
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the stop grace period for the container. This specifies the amount of time to wait before forcing a container to stop after the stop or shutdown signal is sent. The value can be defined in a time duration format, such as "10s" for 10 seconds or "1m" for 1 minute.

capabilities.name: stopSignal
capabilities.description: Specifies the signal that will be used to stop the container. This property allows you to define a custom stop signal other than the default SIGTERM.

This property allows customization of specific Linux kernel settings (sysctl parameters) for the container, enabling fine-tuned control over its behavior. Unsupported parameters will result in an error. Example: Use this property to set parameters like `net.ipv4.tcp_syncookies` or `net.core.somaxconn`.

This property can also include named volumes as defined in the Compose file's top-level `volumes` section.
Volumes can specify additional options such as read-only access or volume drivers.

handleTypes.name: ServiceDependency
handleTypes.description: Represents a service dependency in a Docker Compose file.

capabilities.name: condition
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the condition under which the service should be started.

capabilities.name: setCondition
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the condition under which the service should be started.

handleTypes.name: ConfigReference
handleTypes.description: Represents a reference to a configuration used within a service resource.
handleTypes.remarks:
This class is typically used to define configurations associated with a
service in a containerized environment. It includes information about
the configuration source, target, ownership, and permissions.

capabilities.name: gid
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the group ID (GID) used to identify the group of the referenced configuration.
capabilities.remarks:
The GID is an optional integer parameter that specifies the group ownership
for the resource. If set, it defines the group to which the target resource belongs.

capabilities.name: mode
capabilities.description: Represents the access mode for the configuration reference in the form of an integer value. This property determines the permissions or access level for the configuration being referenced. Typical values might correspond to standard file permission modes.

capabilities.name: setGid
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the group ID (GID) used to identify the group of the referenced configuration.
capabilities.remarks:
The GID is an optional integer parameter that specifies the group ownership
for the resource. If set, it defines the group to which the target resource belongs.

capabilities.name: setMode
capabilities.description: Represents the access mode for the configuration reference in the form of an integer value. This property determines the permissions or access level for the configuration being referenced. Typical values might correspond to standard file permission modes.

capabilities.name: setSource
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the source configuration reference. This property specifies the origin of the configuration file or data required by the service node.

capabilities.name: setTarget
capabilities.description: Specifies the target location where the referenced configuration data will be applied or mounted in the context of the Docker service.

capabilities.name: setUid
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the user identifier (UID) associated with the configuration reference. Optional property that specifies the user ID for accessing the configuration target.

handleTypes.name: SecretReference
handleTypes.description: Represents a reference to a secret within a Docker service configuration.

capabilities.name: gid
capabilities.description: Represents the group ID (GID) associated with the secret reference in a Docker service node.
capabilities.remarks: This property defines the group ID of the user for accessing the secret. It is optional and may be null if not specified.

capabilities.name: mode
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the file mode for the secret reference. The mode defines the file permissions that will be applied to the secret when mounted. This is represented as an integer value.

capabilities.name: setGid
capabilities.description: Represents the group ID (GID) associated with the secret reference in a Docker service node.
capabilities.remarks: This property defines the group ID of the user for accessing the secret. It is optional and may be null if not specified.

capabilities.name: setMode
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the file mode for the secret reference. The mode defines the file permissions that will be applied to the secret when mounted. This is represented as an integer value.

capabilities.name: setSource
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the name of the source secret reference. This property is used to specify the source from which a secret or configuration is derived.

capabilities.name: setTarget
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the target path where the secret will be mounted within the container. This path is used to specify the destination location of the secret in the container's file system.

capabilities.name: setUid
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the user ID (UID) associated with the secret reference. This property allows specifying the UID that will be assigned to the secret when it is mounted within a container.

handleTypes.name: Ulimit
handleTypes.description: Represents the configuration for system resource limits (ulimits) for a container.
handleTypes.remarks:
This class is typically used to specify soft and hard limits for resources
such as file descriptors or process count for Docker containers.

capabilities.name: hard
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the hard limit for the resource control.
capabilities.remarks:
The hard limit defines the maximum value that cannot be exceeded.
It is usually set by an administrator and imposes a strict upper boundary.
This property can be null, indicating no hard limit is set.

capabilities.name: setHard
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the hard limit for the resource control.
capabilities.remarks:
The hard limit defines the maximum value that cannot be exceeded.
It is usually set by an administrator and imposes a strict upper boundary.
This property can be null, indicating no hard limit is set.

capabilities.name: setSoft
capabilities.description: Defines the soft limit for the Ulimit configuration. The soft limit is the value for resource restrictions that a process is allowed to increase up to the hard limit. This property is nullable, which indicates that this configuration might not be set.

capabilities.name: soft
capabilities.description: Defines the soft limit for the Ulimit configuration. The soft limit is the value for resource restrictions that a process is allowed to increase up to the hard limit. This property is nullable, which indicates that this configuration might not be set.

handleTypes.name: Volume
handleTypes.description: Represents a volume definition in a Docker Compose configuration file.
handleTypes.remarks:
The `Volume` class is used to define properties and options for volumes in a Docker environment.
Volumes are used to persist data beyond the lifecycle of a container and can be shared among multiple containers.

capabilities.name: driver
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the name of the driver used for the volume. The driver is responsible for managing the volume and its storage backend.

capabilities.name: driverOpts
capabilities.description: Represents a collection of driver-specific options for the volume. These options are passed as key-value pairs to the volume driver, allowing customization or configuration specific to the driver being used.

capabilities.name: external
capabilities.description: Indicates whether the volume is external to the current scope or environment. A value of `true` specifies that the volume is managed outside the scope of the current application or configuration. A value of `false`, or a null value, indicates that the volume is managed internally or by default behavior.

capabilities.name: labels
capabilities.description: Gets or sets a dictionary of labels associated with the volume. Labels are key-value pairs that can be used for metadata purposes or for organizing and identifying volumes within Docker services.

capabilities.name: name
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the name of the Docker Compose member.
capabilities.remarks:
This property is used to uniquely identify the member (e.g., network, service) within
the Docker Compose configuration. It must be explicitly defined for all derived types.

capabilities.name: readOnly
capabilities.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the volume is mounted as read-only.
capabilities.remarks:
When set to true, the volume will be mounted with read-only permissions, preventing modification
of the data within the container. This is commonly used to enforce data immutability for certain use cases.

capabilities.name: setDriver

capabilities.name: setDriverOpts
capabilities.description: Represents a collection of driver-specific options for the volume. These options are passed as key-value pairs to the volume driver, allowing customization or configuration specific to the driver being used.

capabilities.name: setTarget

capabilities.name: setType
capabilities.description: Gets or sets the type of volume. This specifies the method of volume provisioning such as bind mounts, named volumes, or other supported volume types in Docker.
